name: python-kv

metadata.name: python-kv
metadata.summary: Async key-value store ABC. Implementations over SQLAlchemy, the filesystem, Redis, Azure Blob, and more.

documentation:
# KV

`KV` is an async key-value store interface for Python. It provides a simple API to store serializable objects.

```bash
pip install python-kv
```

`KV` supports multiple backends, including the filesystem, SQLite, Redis, Azure Blob, and many more.

```python
from kv import KV

kv = KV.of('sql+sqlite:///path/to/db.sqlite', type=dict) 

await kv.insert('hello', {'world': 42})
await kv.read('hello') # {'world': 42}
await kv.delete('hello')
async for k, v in kv.items():
  ...
await kv.clear()
```

[**Read the docs!**](https://marciclabas.github.io/kv/)

classes.name: kv.impl._dict.DictKV
documentation: In-memory `KV` implementation over a built-in dict

classes.name: kv._abc.KV

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.of
documentation:
Create a KV (Key-Value) store instance from a connection string.

The function supports various schemes to connect to different types of key-value stores,
and allows for additional parameters to be specified via the query string.

Supported schemes:
\- `file://<path>`: FilesystemKV
\- `sqlite://<path>?table=<table>`: SQLiteKV (uses sqlite3)
\- `sql+<protocol>://<conn_str>?table=<table>`: SQLKV (uses SQLAlchemy)
\- `azure+blob://<conn_str>`: BlobKV
\- `azure+blob://<conn_str>?container=<container_name>`: BlobContainerKV
\- `azure+cosmos://<conn_str>?db=<db>`: CosmosKV
\- `azure+cosmos://<conn_str>?db=<db>&container=<container>`: CosmosContainerKV
\- `azure+cosmos://<conn_str>?db=<db>&container=<container>&partition=<partition>`: CosmosPartitionKV
\- `http://<endpoint>?token=<bearer>` or `https://<endpoint>?token=<bearer>`: ClientKV
\- `redis://<url>`, `rediss://<url>`, `redis+unix://<url>`: RedisKV

Examples:
\>>> kv = KV.of('<file://path/to/base?prefix=hello/>')
\>>> kv = KV.of('sqlite://path/to/db.sqlite?table=mytable')
\>>> kv = KV.of('http://example.com?token=secret&prefix=hello-')

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.insert
documentation: Insert entry: `self[key] = value`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.read
documentation: Read item with key `key`. Raises `InexistentItem` if the item does not exist.

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.safe_read
documentation: Read item with key `key`. Returns `None` if the item does not exist.

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.delete
documentation: Delete item with key `key`. Returns `Left[InexistentItem]` if the item does not exist.

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.items
documentation: Iterate over all items in the `KV`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.has
documentation: Does the `KV` have `key`?

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.keys
documentation: Read all keys in the `KV`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.values
documentation: Iterate over all values in the `KV`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.copy
documentation: Copy `self[key]` to `to[to_key]`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.move
documentation: Move `self[key]` to `to[to_key]`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.rename
documentation: Rename `key` to `new_key`

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.clear
documentation: Delete all entries

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.prefixed
documentation: Create a `KV` with all keys prefixed with `prefix`, without nesting.

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.prefix
documentation:
Nested prefix. Supports slashes in `prefix`.
\>>> kv.prefix('a/b')
is equivalent to
\>>> kv.prefixed('a').prefixed('b')

methods.name: kv._abc.KV.served
documentation:
Create a `LocatableKV` assuming `self` is being served at `base_url`

Example:
```
import uvicorn
from kv import KV, ServerKV

host = ...
port = ...
kv = KV.of('file://data').served(f'http://{host}:{port}')
kv.url('hello') # f'http://{host}:{port}/read?key=hello'
api = ServerKV(kv)
uvicorn.run(api, host=host, port=port)
```
Async, exception-free key-value store ABC

classes.name: kv.impl.sql.SQLKV
documentation: `KV` implementation over sqlalchemy

classes.name: kv.impl.redis.RedisKV
documentation: Redis-based `KV` implementation

functions.name: kv.impl.fs.ensure_path
documentation:
Creates the path to `file`'s folder if it didn't exist
- E.g. `ensure('path/to/file.txt')` will create `'path/to'` if needed

functions.name: kv.impl.fs.rec_paths
documentation: Returns all files inside `base_path`, recursively, relative to `base_path`

classes.name: kv.impl.fs.FilesystemKV
documentation: Filesystem-based `KV` implementation

class_variables.name: kv.impl.azure.blob.blob.BlobKV.split_key
documentation: Split a key into container + blob. Defaults to `{container}/{blob/with/slashes}`

class_variables.name: kv.impl.azure.blob.blob.BlobKV.merge_key
documentation: Merge a container and blob into a key. Defaults to `{container}/{blob/with/slashes}`

classes.name: kv.impl.azure.blob.container.BlobContainerKV
documentation: Key-Value store using a single Azure Blob Container. Keys must be valid blob names

class_variables.name: kv.impl.azure.cosmos.db.CosmosKV.split_key
documentation: Split a key into container + partition. Defaults to `{container}/{id/with/slashes}`

class_variables.name: kv.impl.azure.cosmos.db.CosmosKV.merge_key
documentation: Merge a container and partition into a key. Defaults to `{container}/{id/with/slashes}`

class_variables.name: kv.impl.azure.cosmos.container.CosmosContainerKV.split_key
documentation: Split a key into container + partition. Defaults to `{container}/{id/with/slashes}`

class_variables.name: kv.impl.azure.cosmos.container.CosmosContainerKV.merge_key
documentation: Merge a container and partition into a key. Defaults to `{container}/{id/with/slashes}`
